primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup foreign exchange exposures for hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements under IFRS

primary_authority.reason: Establishes the core eligibility requirement that only assets, liabilities, firm commitments or highly probable forecast transactions with parties external to the reporting entity can be designated as hedged items in consolidated financial statements
Provides the specific exception for intragroup monetary items where foreign exchange gains/losses are not fully eliminated on consolidation and may qualify as hedged items

supporting_authority.reason: Clarifies the scope of hedge accounting for foreign currency risks arising from net investments and reinforces the external party requirement
Confirms that hedge accounting may be applied only to foreign exchange differences arising between functional currencies of foreign operation and parent entity

peripheral_authority.reason: Addresses disclosure of intragroup related party transactions and balances but does not govern hedge accounting eligibility
Deals with structured entity disclosures and is not directly relevant to intragroup dividend hedge accounting

treatment_families.family: General prohibition on hedging intragroup items in consolidated financial statements
Exception for intragroup monetary items with uneliminated FX exposure
Net investment hedge accounting for foreign operations

approaches.label: Hedge accounting prohibition for intragroup items
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.5 establishes the general rule prohibiting hedge accounting for transactions between entities within the same group in consolidated financial statements, which would apply to intragroup dividend receivables that are eliminated on consolidation

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item exception
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 paragraph 6.3.6 provides a specific exception permitting hedge accounting for intragroup monetary items where foreign exchange differences are not fully eliminated on consolidation under IAS 21, which could potentially apply if the dividend receivable creates a residual uneliminated FX exposure

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 addresses hedge accounting for foreign currency risks arising from net investments in foreign operations, representing a distinct top-level approach for managing FX exposure that may be relevant if the dividend receivable relates to a foreign operation
